children.heading: .—Briefings Required
children.label: a .—Briefings Required
children.text: The Under Secretary of Defense for Policy, the Commander of United States Cyber Command, and the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, or designees from each of their offices, shall provide to the congressional defense committees quarterly briefings on all offensive and significant defensive military operations in cyberspace, including clandestine cyber activities, carried out by the Department of Defense during the immediately preceding quarter.
An update, set forth separately for each applicable geographic and functional command, that describes the operations carried out in the area of operations of that command or by that command.

An update, set forth for each applicable geographic and functional command, that describes defensive cyber operations executed to protect or defend forces, networks, and equipment in the area of operations of that command.
An update on relevant authorities and legal issues applicable to operations, including any presidential directives and delegations of authority received since the last quarterly update.
An overview of critical operational challenges posed by major adversaries or encountered in operational activities conducted since the last quarterly update.

children.label: i
children.text: using both quantitative and qualitative metrics; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in a way that is common to all military departments; and

children.label: B
children.text: is consistent with readiness reporting pursuant to .section 482 of this title
Any other matters that the briefers determine to be appropriate.

children.heading: .—Documents
children.label: c .—Documents
children.text: Each briefing under subsection (a) shall include a classified placemat, summarizing the elements specified in paragraphs (1), (2), (3), and (5) of subsection (b), and an unclassified memorandum, summarizing the briefing’s contents.
